zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton是Edward Bolton和Mrs Edward Bolton的女兒，於1535年出生在英國藍卡爾吉可哈姆。她和John Sharples至少有7個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1620年在英國藍卡爾博爾頓享年85歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton是Edward Bolton和Mrs Edward Bolton的女儿，于1535年出生在英国藍卡爾吉可哈姆。她和John Sharples至少有7个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1620年在英国藍卡爾博爾頓享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bolton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern England named Bolton (Lancashire, Northumberland, Westmorland, and Yorkshire) or from Boulton in Derbyshire and East Lothian in Scotland, from Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’ (see Bold 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Bold
meanings: English (Lancashire and Staffordshire): from Middle English bold ‘dwelling-house’ (Old English bold the West Midland and northwestern form of Old English bōthl, bōtl), especially in the sense ‘castle or manor house’ for someone who lived or worked at the main house, or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, for example Bold (Lancashire), The Bold in Aston Botterell (Shropshire), Booden Farm in Haughton, Booth in Blithfield, and Bull Bridge in Penkridge (all Staffordshire). Compare Bodle .
English: nickname from Middle English bold, Old English b(e)ald ‘bold, brave, noble’, which in Middle English developed the additional sense ‘arrogant, impudent, shameless’.
English: variant of   Bolt   and possibly a variant of   Bald   2.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton was born in 1535, in Kirkham, Lancashire, England as the daughter of Edward Bolton and Mrs Edward Bolton. She had at least 7 sons and 2 daughters with John Sharples. She died in 1620, in Bolton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton est née en 1535, à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre, fille d’Edward Bolton et Mrs Edward Bolton. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles avec John Sharples. Elle est décédée en 1620, à Bolton, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton wurde 1535 in Kirkham, Lancashire, England, als Tochter von Edward Bolton und Mrs Edward Bolton geboren. Sie hatte mit John Sharples mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1620 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Bolton, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton è nata il nel 1535 nel Kirkham, Lancashire, England come figlia di Edward Bolton e Mrs Edward Bolton. Lei avuto almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie con John Sharples. Morì nel 1620, nel Bolton, Lancashire, England, all'età di 85 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Boltonは1535年にイギリスランカシュアイアキルクハムでEdward BoltonとMrs Edward Boltonの娘として生まれた。彼女はJohn Sharplesとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1620年にイギリスランカシュアイアボルトンで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton(은)는 1535년 영국 랭카실르 커카에서 Edward Bolton(와)과 Mrs Edward Bolton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 John Sharples(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1620년 영국 랭카실르 볼턴에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton nasceu em 1535, em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra filha de Edward Bolton e Mrs Edward Bolton. Ela teve pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas com John Sharples. Ela faleceu em 1620, em Bolton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton родилась в 1535 г. в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия. Её родителей звали Edward Bolton и Mrs Edward Bolton. У неё было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери от John Sharples. Она умерла в 1620 г. в местности болтон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bolton nació en 1535, en Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra como hija de Edward Bolton y Mrs Edward Bolton. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas con John Sharples. Murió en 1620, en Bolton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años.
